title: Pakistan Field Marshal Munir in Tehran; No Date Yet for Second US-Iran Round in Islamabad
detail: Pakistan's Field Marshal Asim Munir was in Tehran on April 16, 2026, meeting with Iranian Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf and President Masoud Pezeshkian to deliver messages from the United States and lay the groundwork for a second round of negotiations. Pakistan's Foreign Ministry confirmed that the US and Iran were in discussions through Islamabad to hold a second meeting between their negotiators but no date had been set. Al Jazeera reported that Pakistan was pushing to keep diplomacy alive despite an impasse on Iran's enriched uranium stockpile and nuclear program scope. Pakistan's role as the sole communications channel between Washington and Tehran in the nuclear crisis gave Islamabad extraordinary leverage — a position unprecedented in Pakistan's history that directly elevated its global standing in ways that sharply contrasted with India's exclusion from the process.

media.caption: Iranian Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf meets Pakistan Field Marshal Munir in Tehran, April 16, 2026

title: Indian Intelligence on High Alert as Pakistan Allegedly Pushes False Flag Narrative Ahead of Pahalgam Anniversary
detail: Indian intelligence agencies were placed on high alert ahead of the April 22 first anniversary of the Pahalgam tourist attack. WION News reported that Pakistani intelligence was allegedly running a coordinated propaganda campaign claiming India would stage a 'false flag' operation around the anniversary to justify a new military strike. India's security establishment viewed the Pakistani false flag narrative as a pre-emptive information operation designed to deter India from any military options it might be considering around the anniversary. Indian defence forces launched joint search operations in border areas of Rajbagh and Ramkote in Kathua district following reports of suspicious terrorist movement. The Daily Pioneer reported that security had been significantly enhanced along the International Border and the Line of Control, with terrorist handlers in Pakistan reportedly attempting to push armed militants across the LOC ahead of the anniversary.

media.caption: Pakistan allegedly running false flag narrative as Indian security forces on high alert before Pahalgam anniversary
